decision_summary: The current design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85.0°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader width to enhance lateral heat distribution towards the cooled boundaries. This is a conservative geometric change within allowed bounds.

changes.reason: Widening the heat spreader increases the surface area for lateral heat conduction towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries (25°C), reducing thermal resistance and lowering the peak chip temperature.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader
Increased heat flux towards side boundaries
risk_notes: Approaching the right domain boundary (x=1.2); ensure the spreader does not exceed domain limits in future steps.
Diminishing returns may occur if the spreader becomes too wide relative to the heat source location.
